name: Phase Shifter
description: This algorithm is based on the Phase Shifter algorithm on the disting mk4. You may like to view the video on that algorithm, which is here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MKjDCUPGxvU. The algorithm is a phase shifter – it generates two outputs, one of which is in a fixed phase relationship to the other. Unlike the Phaser algorithm (see below), which is simply designed to create a swooshy sound, this algorithm is designed for mathematical accuracy, and can produce some interesting psycho-acoustic effects. It can also produce swooshy sounds.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: The output gain applied to the effected signal.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of busses to process, starting from the\nInput. For example, for a stereo signal, set this to 2.

parameters.name: Unshifted mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as\ndescribed above for the unshifted output.

parameters.name: +ve shift mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as\ndescribed above for the positive shifted output.

parameters.name: -ve shift mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as\ndescribed above for the negative shifted output.

input_ports.name: Shift input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the phase shift CV, scaled as 5V\nper 360°.

output_ports.name: Unshifted output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the unshifted output signal. Note\nthat this is not the same as the dry signal unless the\ninput is a pure sine wave.

output_ports.name: +ve shift output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the positive shifted output.

output_ports.name: -ve shift output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the negative shifted output.
